name: Robbery / Ambush
description: Unlike Threats, which are hostile forces moving through the landscape, a Robbery or Ambush targets the fellowship specifically. Someone has watched the road and chosen them.

bandits.label: Bandits
bandits.description: Human outlaws — experienced, organised, willing to bargain but prepared to fight.

events.label: Bandits stepping from cover ahead — a dozen, bows drawn. Their captain demands coin for passage; they look practised.
events.on_eye: Others are behind the fellowship; the trap is sprung.
events.notes: Negotiation event. Paying is expedient; fighting is costly; parley may yield surprises.

events.label: Arrows from the treeline — a bandit ambush, well-chosen ground, the fellowship caught in the open.
events.on_eye: The first volley finds a target; a companion falls wounded.
events.notes: Combat event. Lookout's Awareness roll determined whether surprise was avoided.

events.label: Bandits at night — slipping into camp while the watch drowses. Not a frontal attack; a theft, perhaps a kidnapping.
events.on_eye: Something crucial is gone in the morning — a pack, a companion, a weapon.
events.notes: Watch event. Successful Awareness spots them; failure means theft is accomplished.

orc_ambush.label: Orc Ambush
orc_ambush.description: Not a patrol moving past — a deliberate attack prepared for the fellowship itself.

events.label: Orcs springing from concealment — a dozen or more, no warning, a carefully-chosen killing-ground. The fellowship is the prey.
events.on_eye: There is a captain among them — a larger, better-armed figure. The fight will be harder.
events.notes: Combat event. Awareness determines whether the first blow lands.

events.label: The road ahead has been prepared — a tree felled, a pit concealed, stones rigged to fall. The fellowship is herded toward a trap.
events.on_eye: The trap claims a companion; gear or life at risk.
events.notes: Investigation and combat event. Spotting the trap saves lives.

desperate.label: Starving Youth
desperate.description: Not bandits by trade but by necessity — the desperate, the hungry, the refugees.

events.label: A group of starving young people stepping into the road — thin, ragged, armed with knives and farm-tools. They do not want to fight; they want food.
events.on_eye: Violence breaks out despite their reluctance; blood that should not have spilled.
events.notes: Moral event. Compassion costs rations; refusal may still cost fighting.

events.label: A child, perhaps two, glimpsed fleeing with a pack — thieves, but thieves of the cold kind, driven by hunger. They will not stand and fight.
events.notes: Awareness event. Pursuit possible; catching them is the small problem. Deciding what to do is the larger.

events.label: A desperate family on the road — mother, father, several children, no fit protector. They do not threaten, but they ask, and their asking is a kind of demand.
events.notes: Moral event. Aid is a choice; the fellowship's generosity may echo later.
